rationale: The citing paper uses Dhar and Simonson (2003) repeatedly in its core theoretical framing, not just in a background list. It relies on that work to establish that choice difficulty drives both deferral and compromise effects, then positions its own contribution as showing similar effects via metacognitive preference fluency while holding option attributes and sets constant. This citation therefore functions as a direct prior line that the 2007 study builds on conceptually and empirically.
relation_description: The 2007 paper builds on Dhar and Simonson (2003)'s account of deferral and compromise as outcomes of difficult trade-offs. It extends that line by introducing preference fluency as an additional, extraneous-to-content driver of the same choice patterns.
